description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular emergency bandage package, commonly used as a first-aid kit component. The front side features clear printed labeling, including the words 'THE EMERGENCY BANDAGE' and detailed usage instructions, all in white text on a dark green, military-style plastic wrapper. The packaging appears to be vacuum-sealed, giving it a flat, slightly rigid appearance with sealed edges and a central, slightly bulging section where the bandage is stored. The back side is plain, with no visible text or markings, and the sides are narrow, showing the thickness of the package. The material is likely a durable, water-resistant plastic, designed to protect the sterile contents inside. This type of emergency bandage is typically found in urban first-aid stations, vehicles, or public safety kits.
description: A compact, dark green emergency bandage package with white labeling and a vacuum-sealed, rectangular shape. The front displays detailed emergency instructions, while the back is plain.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin, sealed edge of the package with minimal visible detail.
description_view_1: The front view displays the emergency labeling, instructions, and product information in white text on a dark green background.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow side view, similar to view 0, showing the other sealed edge of the package.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a plain, unmarked dark green surface with no visible text or symbols.
